ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Jina n nu bɛrɛ na Yesu afa nu ka i yiko n be afite i ŋuniɛ nu akɔ ayo juma. Na wɔ kaaki aniɛ jama n nu, na wɔ bisa ka, “Ŋma faari i saa kaanri m tanlɛɛ n a?”
translated: My name is Bere and Jesus has taken away my strength so that I cannot do any work. And the people gathered and asked, “What has caused you to become like this?”

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Be koro fa má sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ kpa tɔ má tani dawa kunma, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ ni sɔ́n tani dawa n wɔ, na i téke i kunma buka su.
translated: But Jesus answered and said, “You do not understand what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Na Fɛmɛ Herode afere asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n asiri nu na i si saŋga bo asara­mbaa n tiɛɛri ni.
translated: King Herod ordered the soldiers to search for the child and kill him.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo u sa­yoo­wa ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be bo i yo m yarada n be wuraari satiɛ nu ni, i ti má kpa. Nzɛn má bu faari ŋgani san yabuɛ bambaka ciciiri u kɔm na bu fa u kɔ tu tieku nu ka nzue di u, i ti ye tara bo a ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be wura satiɛ nu ni.
translated: Then Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.”

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, “Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan kereeri minɛm ka i ti m susu­fɔ, mini gusu bá kan kere m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n ka i mi ti m dɛɛ wɔ.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “If anyone wants to come with me, he must forget himself and take up his cross every day and follow me.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: Kabo bu tiɛntiɛɛnri jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n nɔɔ, na bu yaraari i ni, sɔ ye i bá yo durunya n awieeri a.
translated: When you are walking on the road and you see a snake, you should not say that you will jump over it and pass.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Yohane wuraari sutura bo bu faari nyɔɔma ndɔrɛ wuuri i a. Na wɔ fa jina abo i wi nu. I diire yo la lalom ni wiɛ wɔ.
translated: John bought new clothes for the festival and wore them. They looked very good on him. He walked proudly through the town.

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Dɔ na wɔ fere bu ka bu su i su, na ba yaki bu si ni, ni i npaadi­fɔm n lee n nu, na ba su i su. Kabo Yesu fɔn yiiri ajini sɔnɔ be ŋu ni
translated: When they had finished eating and drinking, and they got up to leave, they did not thank him, and they left. But Jesus stood up and said to the man who had been healed, “Go home.”

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Jina cɛɛn n ba juuri, minɛ dɔŋgu bá se m ka, ‘Ya Mibiɛ, niɛ e. Ya yo faari u dɛɛ duma, na ya fa akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na ya fa u duma n afɔn ayi ajinim afite minɛm ŋu, na ya kaaki afa u duma n ayo alekutura sam dɔŋgu.’
translated: When the day of the funeral arrives, my uncle will say to me, ‘Mibiɛ’s child, come. Go and tell your father’s brothers that they should come and bury Nyɛmɛ, and that they should bring the cow that is to be slaughtered for the funeral, and that they should also bring the things needed for the funeral.’

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: I hun Yosefu yo ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, nyini ti, wɔ koro má ka i wura i yi n nyinsɛ jama nu, na wɔ fa sunsunni ka i yaki i asiri nu.
translated: When Joseph wanted to leave you and go to another woman, you said that no one would ever know your secret, but the whole world has now learned of your shame.

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu, na wɔ fite ayaki baa ni. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na baa n anya laifiɛ.
translated: Jesus was led into the wilderness, and He fasted for forty days. During that time, He was hungry, and He was tempted by the devil.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Dama kabo am jɔjɔ am beŋgum jɔrɛ jaraki bu ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu jɔjɔ am gusu jɔrɛ jaraki am a. Gɔŋgɔ bo am fa susu ma minɛm ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu fá susu ma am a.
translated: God created the world and everything in it, and he also created man. He gave man authority over everything, and he also gave man authority over himself.

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Nyini sin na Yesu awura lee nu, na i susu­fɔm n asu i su.
translated: You have heard that Jesus is Lord, and you have believed in your heart.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Bɛrɛ bo bu si aŋunfɔ ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá si bu aŋunfɔ a.
translated: When the time comes for the harvest, the groundnuts will be ready, because God has given the harvest.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, haari Fɛmɛ Solomon, ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i yo la i ni, wɔ la má sutura bo i yo nyumi juuri nyini wura pum ni be.
translated: Ama, I heard that you said, Fɛmɛ Solomon's wife, that you are not going to let her stay in your house, and that you will not allow her to eat even a single grain of rice there.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu afite Nazarɛti, miɛ n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, na wɔ ba Yohane dɔ, na Yohane abiɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue Yodan kunma n nu.
translated: Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan River.

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, “Am kpiɛ daafiri, na am sere Nyɛmɛ adena má deke be yo am na am wura satiɛ nu. Ahore­mbaa n koro nyɛmɛsu, ama, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ayɔrɔki.”
translated: And they said to each other, “Let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits; then we will say that a wild animal has devoured him, and we will see what becomes of his dreams.”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Ahin miɛɛnu n bu la ka, minɛm bo ba ka sin kisa ni, bá du mɔ, na bo ba du mɔ kisa n bá ka sin.” Kabo Yesu jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri i yiwee n su kpe nsan su ni
translated: When Jesus had finished saying this, he said to them, “This is how you should pray: ‘Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy. May your kingdom come. May your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.’ Then Jesus went up into the hills to pray.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Kpeŋga na suŋguru n ayasu anati. I yo ti afɔ buru ni nnyɔ baa a. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu woori bɛrɛ n ayo alekutura agaya.
translated: The vulture has no strength to fly. It says that it will not enter the grassland with the other birds. And it is also afraid that it will be caught by the children of the village.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ adi, na bu kunnu ayi. Na i susu­fɔm n atambu kpɔnɔ ni jombaa sinsin n bo bu diiri na i kaari ni, na ba nya kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.
translated: The man went to the farm, and he killed a buffalo. He cut up the meat and put it in a basket, then carried it on his head and came home, and they all ate the meat with porridge.
